title: calcearia mammillifera
intro: Calcearia mammillifera, also known as Sikkim calceolaria or golden bell, is a flowering plant native to the Himalayas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Calcearia mammillifera include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Calcearia mammillifera include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
